meta.win_condition: 3 cases (8-player mode, 4-way simultaneous completion, tiebreaker: fewest excess tokens)

setup.character_assignment: Random draw from bag (base colors). Shuffled order: Nadavch, Adiko, Alex, Yermi, Alonzo, Mai, Shaika, Ilanit
setup.initial_sheriff_reason: Alex has lowest priority (P1) among all 8 characters

actions.thought: Case 19 needs orange+darkred+brown. Mazok has brown and orange - two of three needed tokens. Best opening move.
Case 18 needs blue+brown+green. Nadav HQ has all three colors I need! Simple choice.
Case 16 needs blue+orange+darkred. Barka has blue and darkred. As sheriff, even in collision I pick first.
Case 9 needs orange+darkred+green. Rosa has triple orange! Maximum tokens even if I only need one.
Case 15 needs 2green+brown. Subaru has 2 green tokens! Perfect first move to get half my case done.
Case 14 needs blue+darkred+orange. Dizengoff has 2 darkred - grab one now while they last.
Case 4 needs 2darkred+green. Dizengoff is the only darkred-specialized location. Must grab darkred even with collision risk.
Case 10 needs 2blue+green. Eilat has 3 blue! Going alone means I take 2 blue in one shot.

collisions.resolution_rule: 2-player collision, no sheriff. Priority order: Shaika(P6) first, Mai(P2) second. Each takes 1 token from 2 available.

sheriff_transfer.reason: Tie at 0 cases. Michal2(Mai P2) and Yossi2(Shaika P6) tied at 1 token each. Lower priority (P2) gets sheriff.

outcomes: Collision at Dizengoff: Yossi2(P6) takes darkred, Michal2(P2) takes darkred. All other locations uncontested. No completions.

actions.thought: Need darkred to complete Case 19. Dizengoff has 2 darkred. Going there even with collision risk.
Need brown for Case 18. Mazok has brown. Simple and direct.
Need orange for Case 16. Rosa has orange and brown - alone I take both, complete my case!
Need darkred for Case 9. Dizengoff is the darkred spot! Going in hot.

ability_triggered.description: Empty-handed in 3-way collision (P3 lowest). Draws 2 from bag.

actions.thought: Need brown for Case 15. Nadav has brown and green. Alone means I complete!
As sheriff, I dominate any collision. Subaru has orange and blue - both needed for Case 14!
Need 1 more darkred for Case 4. Dizengoff has 2. Even in collision, P6 priority should get me one.
Need green for Case 10. Barka has 2 green! Alone I take both and complete!

collisions.resolution_rule: 3-way collision, no sheriff. Priority: Nadavch(P8) takes 1, Shaika(P6) takes 1, Yermi(P3) gets nothing -> ability triggers.

sheriff_transfer.reason: Ronit(0 cases) and Yossi2(0 cases) tied. Yossi2 has 2 tokens vs Ronit's 4. Yossi2(Shaika P6) gets sheriff.

outcomes: EXPLOSIVE ROUND: 6 completions! 3-way collision at Dizengoff. Yermi ability triggers (draws darkred+brown from bag). Only Ronit and Yossi2 did not complete a case.

referee_flags.content: 6 out of 8 players completed a case in Round 2. 75% completion rate in a single round is extremely high. Indicates that the 8-player setup with diverse board tokens makes first cases trivially easy.

Yermi ability (draw 2 from bag) triggered in 3-way collision at Dizengoff. Draws were darkred+brown - neither was the green Ronit needed. Bag draw randomness can be frustrating when you need a specific color.

actions.thought: Case 17 needs 2green+orange. Subaru should have green and orange-ish tokens. Alone = take 2.
Case 7 needs green+darkred+blue. Eilat has blue and darkred - two of my needs in one trip.
Case 5 needs orange+blue+brown. Rosa has orange and blue! Both needed tokens in one location.
I desperately need green for Case 9. Normal locations are risky. Closet bag-draw is chaotic but maybe I get lucky!
Case 20 needs 2blue+darkred. Eilat has blue and darkred. Even in collision I get one.
Case 13 needs 2orange+brown. Mazok has brown and orange. Get one of each.
As sheriff, I take 2 at any location. Dizengoff has 2 darkred but I need green for Case 4. Wait, it has green+darkred now. Perfect!
Case 1 needs orange+brown+green. Mazok has brown and orange. Collision with Michal2 but my P7 beats her P2.

collisions.resolution_rule: 2-player, no sheriff. Alonzo(P5) first, Adiko(P4) second. Each takes 1.
2-player, no sheriff. Ilanit(P7) first, Mai(P2) second. Each takes 1.

sheriff_transfer.reason: Ronit(0 cases) and Yossi2(1 case now). Ronit has fewest cases (0) but 6 tokens. No one else at 0. Actually ronit is only one with 0 cases. Sheriff -> Ronit? No wait. Fewest cases = Ronit(0). Sheriff to player-4(Ronit/Yermi).

outcomes: Ronit Messy Closet drew darkred+blue+orange - no green again! Kept orange+darkred. Yossi completes 2nd case. Yossi2 completes 1st case with sheriff advantage at Dizengoff.

referee_flags.content: Ronit (Yermi, chaotic) went to Messy Closet seeking green. Drew darkred+blue+orange - no green in draw. With only 1 green remaining in bag out of 10 tokens, green starvation is real. Ronit now has 6 tokens in hand with 0 cases completed.
Eilat Reef collision: Danny2(P5) picked blue, Michal(P4) picked darkred. Priority order worked as expected. Both got useful tokens.

actions.thought: Case 17 needs 1 more green. Barka has green. Alone = take 2, complete and move on.
Case 7 needs blue. Eilat has 2 blue! Perfect - alone I take both and complete my case!
Case 8 needs 2brown+darkred. Mazok has brown. Start collecting.
As sheriff, I dominate any collision. Nadav has 2 green! Finally! Take 2 green tokens!
Case 20 needs darkred. Dizengoff has green+darkred. Alone I take both.
Case 13 needs 2orange+brown. Rosa has orange. Getting closer to completion.
Case 2 needs 2orange+blue. Nadav might have orange. Worth going even with Ronit (sheriff) there.
actions.empty_handed_reason: Sheriff (Ronit) took both tokens at Nadav HQ. Only 2 tokens available, sheriff entitled to 2.

actions.thought: Case 1 needs brown. Subaru has brown+blue. Alone I take both and complete!

collisions.resolution_rule: 2-player, sheriff present (Ronit). Sheriff takes 2 (green+orange), rival Yossi2 gets 0 (location empty). Yossi2 empty-handed.

cases_drawn.note: Last card in deck before Ronit2

sheriff_transfer.reason: Yossi2(1 case, 1 token) trailing. Gets sheriff.

outcomes: 4 completions. Ronit FINALLY gets green from Nadav HQ using sheriff advantage. Yossi2 empty-handed at Nadav collision - ability timing conflict noted. Deck exhausted - Ronit and Ronit2 have no private cases.

referee_flags.content: Shaika Trail Tracker ability timing conflict: ability text says 'peek at other players choices before reveal and may change own.' But trigger is 'empty-handed after collection.' These are contradictory timings. The peek+change must happen BEFORE reveal, but empty-handed status is only known AFTER collection. Recommend clarifying in GSD: either change trigger to a voluntary pre-reveal activation, or change ability effect to something post-collection.
Sheriff at 2-token location (Nadav HQ): sheriff took both tokens, rival got 0. This is particularly punishing when the location only has 2 tokens. The rival has no recourse.
Case deck exhausted after Round 4. Ronit and Ronit2 have no private cases. Both must rely on public cases (11 or 6) to reach 3 cases. This is a significant disadvantage.

actions.thought: Case 12 needs green+brown+darkred. Eilat has green+brown! If alone, I take both and only need to find darkred... wait, I HAVE darkred! ALONE AT EILAT = WIN!
Case 3 needs darkred+brown+blue. Subaru has brown+blue. Alone means I get both... but still need darkred.
Case 8 needs 2brown+darkred. Barka has darkred+blue. Get darkred, then need 1 more brown.
No private case. Public Case 11 needs brown+blue+orange. I have brown+orange already. Nadav has blue! Go get it.
Case 20 needs 2blue+darkred. Have 1 blue + 2 darkred. Barka has darkred+blue. Get blue to complete!
Case 13 needs 2orange+brown. Eilat has brown. I need brown to complete. Even collision gives me a shot.
As sheriff, I take 2 at Nadav. Case 2 needs 2orange+blue. Nadav has blue+orange. Sheriff bonus!
No private case. Public Case 11 needs brown+blue+orange. I have blue. Mazok has green+orange. Get orange.

collisions.resolution_rule: 2-player, no sheriff. Nadavch(P8) first, Mai(P2) second. Each takes 1.
2-player, no sheriff. Alonzo(P5) first, Alex(P1) second. Each takes 1.

sheriff_transfer.reason: Ronit(1 case, 5 tokens) and Yossi2(1 case, 1 token) tied at fewest cases. Yossi2 fewer tokens. Keeps sheriff.

outcomes: Danny collided with Michal2 at Eilat - only got green, not brown. Cannot complete Case 12 this round. Yossi got darkred at Barka but needs 1 more brown. Danny2 and Michal2 complete their 2nd cases. Ronit empty-handed again at Nadav (sheriff collision).

referee_flags.content: Danny collision at Eilat prevented what would have been a Round 5 win. Shows that 8-player collision density naturally creates kingmaking prevention - other players unwittingly block leaders.
6 players now at 2 cases. Race is extremely tight. Next round likely produces winner.

actions.thought: Case 12 needs green+brown+darkred. I have darkred+green. Eilat has brown+darkred. ALONE = take brown = WIN. Avoiding others.
Case 3 needs darkred+brown+blue. I have brown+blue. Rosa has green+darkred. Alone = take darkred = WIN!
Case 8 needs 2brown+darkred. I have brown+darkred+blue. Barka has brown+green. ALONE = take brown = WIN!
No private case. Public Case 11 needs brown+blue+orange. I have brown+orange. Need blue. But sheriff collision risk...
No private case. Public Case 6 needs 2green+orange. I have green+darkred. Need green+orange. Mazok has orange+blue. Get orange.
No private case. Public Case 6 needs 2green+orange. I have green. Dizengoff has green+darkred. Get second green.
As sheriff, take 2 at Nadav. Case 2 needs 2orange+blue. Nadav has 2 orange! Sheriff takes both!
Public Case 11 needs brown+blue+orange. I have blue+orange. Subaru has blue+brown. ALONE = take brown = WIN!

collisions.resolution_rule: 2-player, sheriff (Yossi2). Sheriff takes 2 (orange+orange), Ronit gets 0. Ronit empty-handed (ability already used R2).

tiebreaker_1.rule: Fewest excess tokens in hand after completion

simultaneous_win.winner_reason: Danny (Nadavch) wins with fewest excess tokens (1) vs all others (2)

outcomes: FOUR-WAY SIMULTANEOUS WIN! Danny, Michal, Yossi, and Ronit2 all complete their 3rd case in Round 6. Tiebreaker: Danny wins with only 1 excess token (darkred) vs 2 for everyone else. Ronit2 notably won via public Case 11. Yossi2 also completes Case 2 this round (2nd case) but is not in the race.

referee_flags.content: 4-way simultaneous win in Round 6. Tiebreaker 1 (fewest excess tokens) resolved it cleanly. However, this scenario is likely to occur frequently at 8 players since many players reach 2 cases around the same time. The tiebreaker system WORKS but the 'winning moment' feels diluted when 4 players win together.

Public Case 11 successfully completed by Ronit2 (Ilanit) for the winning case. Public cases are a viable and important path at 8 players, especially when the deck runs out. This validates the 2 public cases design.

Ronit (Yermi, chaotic, P3) finished LAST with only 1 case. Struggled with green starvation for 3 rounds, had 5-6 excess tokens throughout. Low priority + chaotic personality + bad luck = worst outcome. However, the sheriff mechanic (catch-up) gave Ronit the sheriff for Round 4 which finally got green. Catch-up worked but was too late.
Game lasted 6 rounds at 8 players. Very fast. Total completions across 6 rounds: R1(0), R2(6), R3(2), R4(4), R5(2), R6(5) = 19 completions from 20 case cards. Nearly every case card was used.

final_analysis.collision_note: Lower than expected because 8 locations spread players well. Many rounds had 0-1 overlaps despite 8 players. Players avoided each other effectively.

yermi_you_know.result: Drew darkred+brown from bag. Useful tokens but not the green needed. Ability consumed.

nadavch_order_in_town.note: Danny never went empty-handed thanks to P8 priority advantage.

ilanit_full_case.note: Ronit2 never went empty-handed thanks to P7 priority.

shaika_trail_tracking.note: TIMING CONFLICT: Ability should trigger when empty-handed but its effect (peek before reveal) requires pre-collection timing. Yossi2 went empty-handed in R4 but ability could not logically activate. See F-005.

final_analysis.ability_note: Only 1 of 8 abilities triggered in 6 rounds. At 8 players with 8 locations, collisions that leave high-priority players empty-handed are rare. Most empty-handed instances happen to LOW priority players, whose abilities are the strongest (by design), but low-priority players are the ones LEAST likely to go empty-handed in 2-player collisions because they get 1 token. They only go empty-handed in 3+ collisions, which were rare. Catch-22 of the ability system.
final_analysis.catch_up_note: Sheriff transferred 5 times across 6 rounds. Effectively gave trailing players priority advantage. Ronit (0 cases through R3) got sheriff in R4 and immediately completed Case 9. System works.
final_analysis.deck_note: 20-card deck exhausted in Round 4 (after 18 draws: 8 initial deal + 2 public + 8 completion draws). Two players (Ronit, Ronit2) had no private case from R4 onward. Public cases became critical path.
final_analysis.public_cases_note: Public Case 11 completed by Ronit2 for the winning case. Public Case 6 attempted by Danny2 and Michal2 but not completed. Public cases are viable at 8p.
final_analysis.green_starvation: Ronit spent 3 rounds (R1-R3) unable to find green tokens. Messy Closet drew darkred+blue+orange instead of green. Bag had only 1 green out of ~12 tokens. Token color distribution in the bag can create frustrating scarcity situations.
final_analysis.game_arc: Fast start (6 completions R2), steady middle (2-4 completions R3-R5), explosive finish (4-way win R6). Game builds tension effectively even at 8 players.
final_analysis.winner_profile: Danny (strategist, Nadavch P8) won via tiebreaker with efficient resource management. High priority (P8) meant never going empty-handed. Strategist personality led to optimal location choices throughout.
